perturbation.original_text: This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Premier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Premier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement").
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Premier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Premier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement"). Any disputes arising out of or relating to this agreement shall be resolved through mandatory arbitration, waiving the right to a jury trial.
perturbation.explanation: Adding a mandatory arbitration clause with a jury trial waiver without explicitly mentioning the consumer's rights to seek legal recourse in court or through other means introduces a legal risk. This addition could be interpreted as an attempt to limit the consumer's ability to pursue claims related to the manufacturing agreement effectively.
perturbation.contradicted_law: The right to jury trial is protected under the Seventh Amendment to the United States Constitution.
perturbation.law_explanation: The added clause on mandatory arbitration and jury trial waiver contradicts the consumer's right to a jury trial as protected by the Seventh Amendment. While arbitration is permissible, the lack of explicit informed consent and clear waiver of the right to a jury trial can render the clause unenforceable, especially concerning consumer agreements.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a mandatory arbitration clause with a jury trial waiver, which potentially contradicts the Seventh Amendment right to a jury trial, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. The scraped law snippet from the Legal Information Institute states that the Seventh Amendment preserves the right to trial by jury in civil claims. While arbitration is not inherently illegal, the Perturbation Explanation suggests the modification introduces a legal risk by potentially limiting a consumer's ability to pursue claims effectively. The added clause, without clear informed consent, may render the clause unenforceable, particularly in consumer agreements, and therefore contradicts the consumer's right to a jury trial as explained.
